game_description: Hunt and sink your opponent's fleet on a hidden grid, 2 players.
filter_1_label: Has opponent found one of your ships?

strategies.strategy_detailed: A skilled opponent's heat map peaks at the center, so the middle is hit first. Bias ships toward the lower-probability edges, never cluster them — keep a 1-cell gap so one hit can't expose a neighbor — and vary your layout each game to stay unpredictable.
strategy_stealth: Center is the hottest target — bias to edges
Never cluster — keep a 1-cell gap
Vary your layout each game

strategies.strategy_detailed: The 5-square Carrier can't truly hide — it covers so many cells the probability map finds it eventually. Don't speed that up: keep it off the center (where a strong opponent fires first) and clear of your other ships. Edge-leaning cells are lower-density, so the Carrier lasts longer there.
strategy_stealth: Carrier can't hide — keep it off-center
Edge-leaning = lower density = lasts longer
Keep it clear of your other ships

strategies.strategy_detailed: Never let two ships touch — not even diagonally. A hit on one leaks info: if opponent shoots adjacent and misses, they prune fewer cells when ships are isolated. Random placement with a 1-cell buffer around every ship is near-optimal against probability-density opponents.
strategy_stealth: No ships touching, even diagonal
1-cell buffer around each ship
Isolated ships are harder to probability-map

strategies.condition: Opening moves with no hits yet
strategies.strategy_detailed: Use a checkerboard (parity) pattern — shoot only squares of one color on a mental chessboard overlay. The smallest ship is 2 squares, so every ship must occupy at least one 'white' square. Parity halves the search space without losing information.
strategy_stealth: Checkerboard pattern cuts search space
Every ship spans at least one color
Parity is optimal before any hits

strategies.condition: Opponent opening moves reveal a pattern
strategies.strategy_detailed: If an opponent sweeps edges or corners in their first 5 moves, they're using a common heuristic. Adapt: if you have ships near edges, move mentally to whether they'll finish the edge sweep or pivot inward after finding nothing.
strategy_stealth: Edge-sweepers pivot inward after nothing
Anticipate their next sweep row
Adjust your shots to their pattern

strategies.condition: Opponent gets a hit on your ship
strategies.strategy_detailed: Once hit, the attacker probes the orthogonally adjacent cells. A ship against a wall leaves only 2 of those directions; a corner leaves 1 — so rim ships are harder to finish. Combined with edges being lower-density (a probability player shoots them later), the rim hides well.
strategy_stealth: Hit on a wall: 2 hunt directions, not 4
Hit in a corner: only 1 direction
Rim ships are lower-density AND hard to finish

strategies.strategy_detailed: Undiscovered ships let you play offensively without psychological pressure. Continue parity shooting without bias — don't cluster shots near your own ships' locations out of anxiety. Your placement is already set; trust it.
strategy_stealth: Stay on parity pattern
Don't bias shots near own ships
Trust placement — play offense

strategies.condition: Hit confirmed, ship not yet sunk
strategies.strategy_detailed: After a hit, shoot orthogonally adjacent squares next turn. Once you confirm orientation (second hit), extend along that axis. Only deviate from the axis when you've missed both ends — then the ship occupies the middle segment.
strategy_stealth: Adjacent squares after first hit
Confirm orientation on second hit
Missed both ends = ship in middle

strategies.condition: Multiple ships unsunk, no active hunt
strategies.strategy_detailed: Probability Density Function (PDF) map: for each unshot cell, count valid placements of remaining ships through it. Shoot highest-count cell, recompute after every shot. Hunt mode: misses and sunk ships are blockers — no placement can overlap them. Beats parity long-term.
strategy_stealth: Count valid placements per cell
Misses/sunk = blockers in placement count
Shoot highest-count cell, recompute every shot

strategies.condition: Active hit, refining the probability map
strategies.strategy_detailed: PDF = count valid ship placements through each cell; shoot highest. On a hit, rules shift: hit squares become passable (ships can overlap them) and placements covering hits score heavily. Skip directions where no ship fits to the hit.
strategy_stealth: After a hit, score placements through it
Hit cells become passable, not blockers
Skip directions no ship can reach it

strategies.condition: A ship has been sunk — adjusting hunt stride
strategies.strategy_detailed: Stride = length of smallest surviving ship. While Destroyer (2) is alive: 2-stride (checkerboard). Destroyer sunk: 3-stride — (row+col)%3==0. Both 3-length ships sunk: 4-stride. Only Carrier left: 5-stride, 20 cells cover the board. Upgrade stride every time the minimum ship length increases.
strategy_stealth: Stride = min surviving ship length
Destroyer sunk → 3-stride
Carrier only → 5-stride, 20 cells total

strategies.strategy_detailed: Finish sinking a found ship before switching to the other. Leaving two active hunts splits your attention and wastes shots testing wrong orientations. One complete sink narrows the remaining fleet and updates your probability map more cleanly.
strategy_stealth: Finish one sink before starting another
Two hunts = split attention
Complete sink = cleaner probability update

strategies.condition: Opponent announces a ship is sunk
strategies.strategy_detailed: When SUNK is called, count hit pegs in cluster vs announced ship length. Match = resolved, return to hunt. More pegs than ship length = adjacent ship hiding there. Target extra peg's orthogonal directions. Ships can legally touch — this is an intentional deception tactic.
strategy_stealth: Count hit pegs vs announced ship length
Mismatch = adjacent second ship in cluster
Target extra peg's orthogonal directions

strategies.strategy_detailed: With 1-2 ships left, calculate exactly where they can fit given all misses. Eliminate impossible squares mentally. The final ship is often in the quadrant with the fewest shots — a common unconscious placement bias. Check that area first.
strategy_stealth: Calculate exact fit zones from misses
Check least-shot quadrant first
Placement bias = cluster in unused area

strategies.strategy_detailed: When opponent's last ship is unknown, revert to parity if no active hit. Don't waste shots in already-eliminated zones. If you've cleared 80%+ of the board, the remaining ship must be in a specific cluster — list all valid positions and shoot the center of the densest.
strategy_stealth: Revert to parity without active hit
List all valid remaining positions
Shoot center of densest valid cluster
